task_context.actionable_question: What should I do differently starting next week?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Synthesise all [FE] risk signals into 3–5 prioritised actions. Reference which FE feature triggered each item.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: You have two important actions to focus on in the coming week to enhance your attendance and study planning. These actions are designed to help you stay organized and set clear goals.

Insights: Attendance Planning: By planning next week's attendance in advance and setting reminders, you can improve your consistency in attending learning sessions. This action is important as your current absence rate is 1.0, which indicates a need for better attendance management. | Setting Study Goals: Choosing an upcoming assessment goal and scheduling focused study sessions will help you target your efforts effectively. Your average score is currently 41.25, which is below the target threshold of 70, indicating that focused study could be beneficial.

explanation.summary: You have two important actions to focus on in the coming week to enhance your attendance and study planning. These actions are designed to help you stay organized and set clear goals.

insights.title: Attendance Planning
insights.description: By planning next week's attendance in advance and setting reminders, you can improve your consistency in attending learning sessions. This action is important as your current absence rate is 1.0, which indicates a need for better attendance management.

evidence.context: This action is linked to rule S-T13-R05, which confirms the need for attendance planning.

insights.title: Setting Study Goals
insights.description: Choosing an upcoming assessment goal and scheduling focused study sessions will help you target your efforts effectively. Your average score is currently 41.25, which is below the target threshold of 70, indicating that focused study could be beneficial.

evidence.context: This action is supported by rule S-T13-R02, which emphasizes the importance of setting study targets.

confidence.reason: The data used to generate these actions is complete and reliable.

task_output_contract: Explain only actions already present in prioritized_actions or supported action-rule evidence.
Do not create duplicate recommendations; explanation.recommendations may be empty by design.
For every explained action, connect it to rule_evaluations, action_evidence_links, evidence_items, owner, priority, and time horizon when supplied.
If no supported action exists, say that only when missing_evidence/unsupported_actions/rule output supports it.
Explain only prioritized_actions whose rule_evaluation is triggered, preserving action and rule provenance.
If prioritized_actions is empty, explicitly state that no supported action was triggered.
v3_warnings: Task-aware V3 prompt exceeded the configured soft token ratio (26.7087 > 1.2).
Task-aware V3 prompt remained above the experimental safety ratio after optional evidence trimming; raw rows were preserved.

prioritized_actions.action_text: Plan next week's attendance in advance and set a reminder for each scheduled learning session.
claim_limits: Do not infer the reason for absence.
Do not trigger when absence_rate is null.

prioritized_actions.action_text: Choose one upcoming assessment goal and schedule two focused study sessions toward the stated target threshold.
claim_limits: Do not promise that the action will achieve the target.
Preserve the runtime threshold values and score scale.

missing_evidence.reason: evidence is unavailable according to availability column

summarization_warnings: Sensitive/background evidence is preserved for audit only and was not used to trigger actions.
The LLM may paraphrase prioritized_actions but must not invent new actions or rules.

required_core_outputs.description: Explain the supported actions already generated or exposed by the action_synthesis rule contract; do not require the explanation model to invent actions outside the returned or triggered action set.
For each supported action that is present, explain the triggering FE feature, threshold or rule evidence when available.

evaluation_constraints.description: Do not include or endorse actions that reference signals not present in returned data or the supplied action-rule contract.
Do not invent risk context, urgency, priority, or additional actions unsupported by returned feature-engineered signals or supported action evidence.

If no supported action is returned or triggered, saying no supported action is available is acceptable; penalize that only when deterministic action evidence shows at least one supported action exists.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
